architecture: RNA-FM (640-dim) + FEGSTrans adapter + 38-dim biophysics, organism-balanced sampler

training.pool: v5/v6 corpus + 83 de-leaked matched training pairs (1396 pos / 678 neg / 184 struct-neg = 2258 total)
training.matched_pairs: 83 synthetic flank-sequestration pairs on 20 diverse G4 cores (A-flank POS vs complementary C/U-flank NEG) + spacer/tract negatives + real motif pairs, de-leaked vs 26 held-out benchmark seqs (max 8-mer Jaccard 0.33)
training.recipe: v6 recipe: unfreeze last 2 backbone layers, lr=1e-4, backbone_lr=5e-6, weight_decay=0.03, patience=6, organism-balanced sampler

cv_performance.note: 5-fold cluster-grouped CV (model/strict_eval_v13_cv/), NO regression vs v6

v6_comparison.note: v13 trades 0.009 overall (within fold noise) for +0.005 non-yeast and the structure-specificity breakthrough below

structure_specificity_benchmark.note: 9 mechanistically matched pairs (same G4 core, one flank/spacer/tract change flips LLPS), HELD-OUT (never seen in training)
structure_specificity_benchmark.flagship: 5'-A9 (LLPS+, 0.94) vs 5'-C9 (LLPS-, 0.69): model now knows C9 flank sequesters the G-core

promotion_rationale: v13 achieves perfect matched-pair ranking (1.00 vs v6 0.67) with zero general-corpus regression. The 83-pair pilot is the sweet spot: v14 (230 pairs) caused -0.02 regression, v15/v16 (downweight variants) traded off other signals. The structure-specificity gap — the primary blind spot — is closed.
threshold_note: F1-optimal on v13 CV is 0.63 (F1=0.863), but 0.50 is nearly equivalent (F1=0.860) and backwards-compatible with v6 users. Use --threshold 0.63 for strict calibration.
